content:
# Pseudo Second Order

**Summary**: A kinetic model used to describe the rate of adsorption, often indicating a chemically-mediated process.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2015-musa|Musa, 2015]] applied the pseudo-second order model to characterize the kinetics of metal ion adsorption.

## Scientific Context

- [[2015-musa|Musa, 2015]] Used to determine if the adsorption process is governed by chemical bonding or physical forces.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2015-musa|Musa, 2015]] Fitting experimental data of qe vs t into the pseudo-second order equation.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The R2 values for all concentrations were >0.99, confirming the chemical nature of the interaction. ([[2015-musa|Musa, 2015, p. 79]])
